reviews.review_title: #1 Vr pirate game & 4 find sword fights
reviews.review_description: I just wanna thank the developers for making this game. It is the best pirate game ever. For the people that are having trouble finding sword, fights, and adventures like those you need to go to taverns and request rumors in order to find these also found out that if you just ride your ship out of the ports into the open sea, you’ll eventually find survivors that are stuck in if you rescue them, then you can get their coordinates to a cave full of treasure. For the developers, I would like to put my thoughts out for the future updates. I think that￼ it would be cool if that you involved the crew on the ship that it would make the game me more. I think that when exploring caves that shone crew members should walk around a throw out certain lines. I also think that when given rum they should look like that they have physical bottle of it unless firing cannons or tending to sailing the ship. Thank you for considering my suggestions and reading my reveiw.

I’ve had this game for about 5 months and the first time a got it I was completely and utterly confused of how to play the game properly. Until now. This game can be lots of fun to play IF you know how to play it properly. There are a lot of cool mechanics and addition’s that add more spice to the game. So if you want the most out of this game please watch videos on YouTube to learn the basic mechanics.

reviews.review_title: Looks fun solo, multiplayer info lacking
reviews.review_description:
Looks like fun and I've enjoyed messing with it in the solo mode.  However I was extremely disappointed when I purchased it as a potential multiplayer friendly option.

Perhaps the listing in the store should be more clear exactly what the multiplayer includes and what it doesn't. The big one being PVE does not include the champaign just the much more limited one-off ship combat modes.  Stating it has pve, pvp, etc just as not enough information.

This game is absolutely awesome in single player. Multiplayer is just naval battles. Basically who can reload quicker. They need to integrate a co-op story line. Either captain a vessel with a friend or you both have your own vessel. Either way I love single player mode but multiplayer got bored really fast. If I'm missing something let me know, this has so much potential. I love this game.

This game is incredibly good it is very fun and because of its Open world setting I can do whatever I want . I would definitely recommend this game if you want a historical pirate game that isn’t filled with cartoon stuff and sea monsters. There are a couple things is would add or change first make a way to buy ships because it is super hard to go up to a ship and try to kill its crew and get it to surrender.  Another thing change the shanties the sea shanties are all ai generated which I’m not so keen on there are loads of sea shanties I would be happy to see such as roll the old chariot or leave her Johnny. The same goes for the art work. And something cool to see and this may be a stretch is having marine type soldiers that you can only hire in country ports and can customize their uniform similar to ships like hat colour coat colour trouser colour shoes and gaiters colour and sling colour and lacing colour. These marines can shoot at enemy’s and take down the crews morale and size. Also the prices for independence are crazy 10,000 okay then goes to 50,000 that’s fine but then it goes straight to 500,000 at most do 100,000 I think 92,000 would be a great price.

The quality of presentation is through the roof.  From the moment you step into the game, you're in beautiful environments. From the cabin of your ship with beautiful wavy glass windows, to the functional map on your desk, to the beautiful views from the deck of the ship. The game operates on its own in-game time as you do your thing, so you'll be seeing all different times of day with beautiful sunny days, sunsets, stormy and cloudy oceans.

The pirate songs that sing throughout the game are also quite catchy! The voicework and soundtrack are outstanding as well.

As far as gameplay, this could be considered a gold standard spiritual influence of Sid Meier's Pirates!/Pirates! Gold. Move along at your own pace if you choose, trading goods for profit to keep your ship sailing, discovering new ports, etc.  Capture lost cargo from the water, dive below the water to shipwrecks to collect spoils, among many other things.

Aside from its beauty and all of the things to do in the satisfying gameplay loop, the game's interface is really well thought out to help the player not to be confused by everything going on. Your map clearly shows you best places to buy or sell goods (after you've discovered them), ship combat gives you two different viewing modes so that you can figure out your own personal preference to handling combat.

And THANK YOU for giving an option to disable spiders in the caves...I know, I'm a wimp.

Congrats to the devs, hope to see lots of success for this game as it's absolutely beautiful, well thought out, super fun and polished game. It's tied together with so many elements working together: Great gameplay mechanics, great UI, great sound, beautiful scenery around every corner.

Congrats!

reviews.review_title: Great game just got a few thoughts
reviews.review_description: This is a fantastic game as is but I would love an option to add more smoke effects and explosion effects and also an option to add  destruction to the forts like parts of the for crumbles and I attack it.The audio could use a little bit of work. Could have a deeper tone to the cannon fire audio and the fact that I can hear an enemy ship sinking from like 200 ft away as if my ship was sinking isn't great. But this game so far is wonderful. And if the islands and tiwns could be just a bit more detailed since you can jump off your ship and walk around on them would be nice

reviews.review_title: Fun and Exciting. . .  to a Point.
reviews.review_description: Very fun game with great  hand to hand combat mixed with sea faring cannon fire. A few flaws here and there. Accidentally hit a button and it ends your current battle. Your own fleet blows themselves up with burning oil  and gun powder kegs. Recalibrating your sextant every 3 minutes. If had to say one thing it's when the governor of Miami wants 500,000 gold pieces for Nassau to be recognized. You start going from port to port saving up gold and when you finally pay him, literally nothing happens. No special ship or weapon upgrade.. just a big nothing. Basically the game is over once you've battled all the pirates and have maxed out your town. Save the 500 000 and upgrade your fleet with it.

Don’t get me wrong the game needs the slightest of work,and it takes a second to learn.But when you get to build Nassau and do prison breaks and fight other ships,it’s really cool.and the ship models are amazing (frigate is my favorite) but once you get into the game it’s so fun.and it’s great story telling.But there are some historical inaccuracies but who cares.But for any one board or likes pirate games,this is for you

Edit 3/14/26….  Is St. Mary’s Passage treasure cave supposed to be blocked off ?   You can’t get to the treasure due to a column right in the way.  You can see it all on the tables etc,  but u can’t get around the wood columns and the one cave column in the center of them.  

Definitely worth it!! 
So much fun. 
You get to captain a ship, 
Command your whole fleet, 
Find treasure in caves, 
Dive underwater shipwrecks for treasure and pearls, 
Battle other ships with your cannons and crew.  Challenge other captains to a duel to have them join your fleet. 
You can store cargo between all of your ships.  Or store it in your warehouse back home. 
Or just sail the seas and come across survivors on rafts needing saved or cargo floating in the water .. 
also if u see big rocks in the middle of the ocean, stop and Search for pearls if shallow water underneath.  The water around the rocks will be a lighter blue color if shallow.   
(Hint:  watch the little fish. They are always attracted to pearls or coins on the bottom of the ocean.  So they will be swimming around them to help you find them between the seaweed) 
Also when you pick up survivors on rafts, they will give locations to caves or ship wrecks.. 

The key is to pay attention to the locations where items are for sale cheaper,   buy them there,  then sell them where they cost more to make a bigger profit 

Seriously it’s so much to do. Just get it 
You won’t regret it I

It takes too long to get 500000 make new ways to make gold I have a good suggestion how about going to capital city's and then take down the defenses and then after that you go to the governor and take the governor's gold

Turns out I was being a bit of a noob 😅,  it was highlighted/noted that the net is exclusively on the right side during that section.

Now that I’ve progressed further, overall the game is genuinely beautiful. The atmosphere, soundtrack and visuals really remind me of Black Flag at times, and sailing around in VR feels awesome.

The combat could use a little more feedback/impact at times, but it’s still satisfying enough overall.

My main complaint now is during combat when going into the menu to transfer merch/equipment between ships or repair my ship. Occasionally I’ll accidentally hit a button that exits the mission back to the map. Even if I cancel it, the enemy ships seem to reset back to full health while my ships retain all the damage taken, which can get pretty frustrating.

I do also have stick drift, which obviously isn’t the developers’ fault and is more of a common Quest/VR controller issue, but it definitely makes accidental menu inputs worse during tense moments.


Otherwise though, the game has a ton of charm and potential. One mechanic I think would be incredibly fun would be boarding enemy ships via grapples and rope swinging across with your crew. Even small rope swing mechanics for cave exploration could add a lot to the pirate fantasy.
The game has no refund wasted 20 bucks for repetitive gameplay horrible steering and naval combat no helpful tutorials and barely any time with melee combat and every item you sell is worthless

developer_response.body:
Please follow these guidelines - https://www.meta.com/en-gb/help/quest/828412189021014/

As a developer, we have no influence on the returns policies established by our partners’ storefronts like Meta Store or Steam.

reviews.review_description:
It’s actually a really decent pirate game. From the resource management system to the map. I wish the distance of sailing would be decreased so I don’t have to rely on fast travel as much. Fast travel kills immersion. I love the cloud systems and how I can be swept off the ship if a big enough swell hits me. It’s just too large of a map to get to missions. Also, maybe adding more things you can find at sea. 

Another cool aspect would be to engage in more close quarters combat, boarding enemy ships instead of fighting with canons, like a real pirate. Can’t defeat them in ship battle because they have too many cannons? Just board it and kill them all. This game has fantastic combat but isn’t utilized enough. It’s really fun to take their weapons and use it against them.

I love the graphics and the potential of this game. I just don't like games that tell you to go into a book and read a hundred different times. I don't want to read every little thing and go to a book. I can do that in real life.... That's not fun in a game. For me, I like things, intuitive and logical in games and action based . Where common sense can shine.... Just a bit too technical for me .... Go to the book and click that. Go to the book and select that go to the book and press a .... yea I'm good on that

This is a great game for the whole family. We find it really immersive, and since there’s building, ship battles, treasure hunting, exploring we all take turns doing different quests. This is the kind of game we thought we’d find more of when we got the meta quest. Great job to developers.

reviews.review_title: DO NOT PLAY IF YOU HAVE STICK DRIFT!!
reviews.review_description: They have it so that you have to turn with your joystick and there is no way to turn it off. If anyone can help me know how to turn it of then help please.

developer_response.body:
You can find the option to calibrate and select appropriate deadzones for your analog sticks in your Meta Quest's system settings. 

It doesn't seem very fair to lower the overall rating based on personal hardware issues.

reviews.review_title: Easily the best VR pirate game
reviews.review_description: This is an amazing pirate game for VR, but there is still a lot of work to do.  Your fleet is filled with morons who will blow themselves up.  The offship combat and graphics are extremely dated.  The sea shanties seem too professional for a group of pirates to be singing.  But I keep coming back to the game.  And you will too.

How do you switch which side the crew aim and fire the cannons? 

I've got the setting set to auto aim and auto fire, but despite the left side of the boat facing the enemy boat, the crew were manning the right hand side cannons (the aiming lines were showing on the right as well) 🫣

I tried looking at the in games help and the Web site FAQ but could find nothing that mentions this aspect of the game.

developer_response.body:
- To quickly switch between ship and battle mode, press the right analog stick.
- To change the cannon's direction during combat, press the Y button on your left controller.
- To quickly change ammo type during combat, press the left analog stick.

Apart from that, you might want to check the "Controls Layout", available under the in-game settings, after pressing the menu (3 horizontal lines) button on your left touch controller. There you will find a comprehensive legend, which should become clear after a bit of experimenting with the interface and both of your touch controllers.

reviews.review_description:
Ok….  So I have not played this long….

But my son and I have been searching for a replacement for Sid Meiers Pirates! ever since I played v1 on my Commodore 64 and he played v2 it on Windows XP.

This game has the vibes!!!

A true VR pirate game/sandbox has been needed.

Caveat….  Graphics….

Not bad, but not great.  

Hoping, HomeNetGames, you port this to PCVR.

You have something special here.  

Tend to it.

Love on it.  

Improve on it.

Invest in it.

This could be the next Blade & Sorcery.  People want Pirates games that provide the whole experience like the original Pirates! did.

Ship sailing, treasure hunting, ship battles, politics, romance, adventure, exploration, fleet building, strategy.

Again, I did not play long, but that’s all it took for me to write this review….  

Oh, and I must admit I started getting a little sea sick.  Not necessarily in a bad way…

Oh, and please allow players to have wives who happen to be the governor’s daughter all over the Caribbean. (IYKYN)

reviews.review_title: most promising pirate experience on meta
reviews.review_description:
This game is an ambitious VR pirate adventure that lets you live out your swashbuckling fantasies on the open sea. Built as a sandbox experience, it combines naval combat, exploration, trading, and base building into a single VR title that feels much larger than the sum of its parts.  ￼

The heart of the game is its open-world Caribbean setting during the Golden Age of Piracy. You begin as a fledgling captain based in Nassau — a historical pirate haven — and your goal is to grow that settlement into a thriving republic of free-spirited privateers. You can sail between ports, engage in naval battles, plunder merchant ships, hunt for treasure, and trade goods for profit.  ￼

This freedom to choose how you want to play — whether as a ruthless pirate raider, a savvy merchant, or a charismatic leader building your fleet — gives the game a sense of scale and possibility rare for a standalone VR title.  ￼

As a VR game, it does a good job of pulling you into its world. Manoeuvring your ship, handling cannons, and navigating Nassau’s harbours all feel tactile and engaging. Sailing across the water with a VR headset on gives a strong sense of presence — you’re not just watching a pirate game, you’re in it.  ￼

The option to view things both from the deck and from an overview map also helps make large-scale navigation and planning more intuitive, blending micro- and macro-level gameplay.  

￼The game features naval combat that rewards strategic choices — different ammunition, traps, and crew management matter in tougher battles — and a basic economy where trading and resource management affect your growth. There’s even underwater treasure hunting, where diving for loot breaks up the sea-bound action.  ￼

These varied systems give Republic of Nassau a strong sandbox feel — there’s always another goal to work toward, another ship to upgrade, or another harbour to visit.  ￼

It’s important to note this game began in Early Access and has been steadily building toward its 1.0 release. Some features are still rough around the edges — story elements are light, and not every mechanic feels fully polished yet — but the foundation shows real ambition.  ￼

The developer has also hinted at future additions like multiplayer, deeper combat systems, and expanded exploration, which could significantly boost the game’s long-term appeal.  ￼

A lot of fun sailing the high seas, you can just breath in the fresh salty air. Up to usual high standard of Home Nets standalone games. Disappointing it is not cross-buy like all their other games I have purchased as this is a game you can really get stuck into without batteries running out over a course of a day.

It is probably the best pirate game so far in vr but only early access
For a vr game there's very little vr play basically just menus most the time.

Open world humm not sure.. 

Missions are so frustrating! I got into a fort to free a prisoner, found the prisoner, found a key that isn't used for anything! Cannot open the prison door!! Apparently the prisoner is supposed to say get the key??? Well he didn't he's just screaming threats to let him out and I've killed all the guards.. So that needs fixing. 

 DO NOT raise the pirate flag in the ships options it is not reversible, everyone just kills you or runs away.

It's tricky to do this game as a vr game, as the menu is probably the fastest way to get things done, trading selling, getting missions etc. 

Mixed feelings about it tbh. In fact if it wasn't for a detailed YouTube video someone did I wouldn't know half of what you are supposed to do in the game.

developer_response.body:
Please consider checking the Codex, where we have compiled a lot of useful information. It is accessible via a button on the book in your cabin.

Regarding the key - holding it in your virtual hand, simply insert it into the lock of the cell where the captain is imprisoned.

reviews.review_description: Message to all noobs don't put the Black flag up at a port with other ships they will see you as hostile and come for your booty and yes I said booty I'm a pirate get with the system and two only bad thing about this game swimming it sucks everyone can agree it is bad also can we get a second campaign that's less about building and more about fighting!

the images with a sword and being able to walk around aren't actually in the game it's just a sailing game and the sailing mechanics are pretty boring at that all of the things that you think you would be able to walk around for or actually just buttons in a menu.

developer_response.body:
All screenshots are captured directly from the game, and you'll simply have to play to discover the activities you're interested in. 

For example, to rescue a jailed captain, you'd have to:
 - Receive a mission by listening to rumors in one of the bandit camps. 
 - Reach the port where the fort is holding the prisoner.
 - Sail your flagship as close to the fort as possible. Stop there.
 - Confirm (A) the message initiating the mission in the fort/prison.
 - After freeing your companion and returning to the room where you started, you'll see a message. After confirming it, you'll return to your ship. 

Another type of mission that offers melee combat is the one involving jungle camps. The starting point for these are also rumors in bandit camps. 

By attacking any military ship docked in the port and causing it to surrender, you have a chance to initiate a boarding attack, which will also involve melee combat. We encourage you to familiarize yourself with the Codex, which you'll find on the desk in the Cabin.

Another place you might want to check is the "Controls Layout", available under the in-game settings, after pressing the menu (3 horizontal lines) button on your left touch controller. There you will find a comprehensive legend, which should become clear after a bit of experimenting with the interface and both of your touch controllers.

If you encounter any issues, please contact our support team at: 
https://www.homenetgames.com/support/

reviews.review_description: This is my favorite game on the Quest. Pirate ship battles. Hunting in caves and shipwrecks for Gold. Sword fights. Build your own port, crew, fleet. Free sailing around the map. Endless fun. The graphics are beautiful. The ship steers and moves like real life. It’s perfect. Great job creating this game. Please continue to improve and make more. Thank you.

Is it me or it is impossible to find the cave entrance at Turtles island? The canoe dropped me at the dive site, so the entrance shouldn’t be too far. I explore the whole area for hours without success. The game seems to have great potential , it’s not fun getting dizzy for nothing. The game should be fun. But I am not enjoying it now.

Edit: Thanks for the prompt response from the dev clearing the confusions. I updated review to 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Exploring caves in search of treasure is an activity you start by accepting the quest message after arriving by ship at the island associated with a specific quest. 

Please check your QUEST LOG to make sure you're in the right place, then head to the cave directly from the ship.

reviews.review_description: I love this game and have been looking for a game like this but hand to hand combat. I’ve been playing for 2 days now and I’ve never encountered hand to hand combat i know you can do it but i don’t know how. When you board a enemy ship is it chance to fight and how do i get prison break and jungle missions I’ve check codex but it isn’t very clear other than that amazing game I would recommend

developer_response.body:
To rescue a jailed captain, you'd have to:
 - Receive a mission by listening to rumors in one of the bandit camps. 
 - Reach the port where the fort is holding the prisoner.
 - Sail your flagship as close to the fort as possible. Stop there.
 - Confirm (A) the message initiating the mission in the fort/prison.
 - After freeing your companion and returning to the room where you started, you'll see a message. After confirming it, you'll return to your ship. 

Another type of mission that offers melee combat is the one involving jungle camps. The starting point for these are also rumors in bandit camps. 

By attacking any military ship docked in the port and causing it to surrender, you have a chance to initiate a boarding attack, which will also involve melee combat. We encourage you to familiarize yourself with the Codex, which you'll find on the desk in the Cabin.

Another place you might want to check is the "Controls Layout", available under the in-game settings, after pressing the menu (3 horizontal lines) button on your left touch controller. There you will find a comprehensive legend, which should become clear after a bit of experimenting with the interface and both of your touch controllers.

reviews.review_description:
Very fun game. It reminds me of the Pirates I used to on NES.  

Three suggestions for improvements:
1. More variety in the governors' missions. These got old really quick.
2. The ability to destroy a fort and choose the nationality of the port. This would make late game fun by being able to change the balance of power.
3. The big one....I'm not sure if this is bug or a feature, but there is a limit of three ships that pops up sometimes...docking at Nassau or the Pirate Hideout (these two I get). It also triggers when you go to the fleet overview in any port or at sea. This can be frustrating.

Still...a wonderful game that gave me hours of enjoyment.

Could be cool someday, but seems very unfinished for now.
Admittedly I didn't play very far (until after the ship fight against Anne Bonny). 
My first gripe is how rather slow and boring the beginning and first impression of the game is. The store page advertises hand-to-hand combat, so you could start with that instead of picking up boxes and going back and forth between spots on a map. The tutorial also felt too 'handholdy' while at the same I didn't really know what do after it was over. Instead of meticulously telling the player every little step and then just stopping, you could at first only give the basics and controls but show little descriptions of whatever menu point your courser is hovering over, so the player can explore and choose more freely while still getting all necessary information. 
When first going from the start point towards Nassau I found a question mark on the map. But after sailing/running around the area for a whole while I couldn't find anything to interact with. A green arrow was pointing towards a fort, but after running around it it didn't even seem to have a door. I don't know if this area is empty or if it's just poorly conveyed how to interact with it. 
One of the biggest problems was how buggy swimming is. I was consistently clipping from too high above the water to under the water. When trying to climb up onto a ship similar things occurred. 
It would also be nice if, in the over ship view, the camera wasn't glued to one side of the ship but instead the ship turned without the camera, like in more conventional 3rd person games. I think that would really help against motion sickness. (Maybe that option is already in the game but I couldn't find it.)
It would be nice to have more information in the fleet overview, like the ships' health.
There are also some visual errors, like flags seem to be on the wrong layer, so that you can always see them even if there should be something blocking the view.

developer_response.body: If you would be so kind as to share your thoughts on potential improvements with us, we would have a chance to improve.

reviews.review_description: I got a meta 3 headset for Christmas, it’s been fun to play around with, but I haven’t found a single game that has been very much fun. Most of the games I get bored with after 30 min. This game is so immersive and fun, and it’s not even finished yet. The developers have done a great job. So may time while I’m playing it I get so excited when I see something new, right when I think I have seen it all. So cool to be able to build your fleet and paint your ships and bar them! And the name shows up on the ship! So cool! Well done, I was starting to think the meta 3 headset was a waste of money. So glad I found this game.

the controls of the ship where odd and i didn't know how to control the sails or stop? horrible controls and you cant even do anything on the ship? not a true pirate game for vr, doesn't even use the vr functionality to control the ship great visuals but boring game, Not For pirates of any form!!!

developer_response.body: The in-game tutorial guides the player step-by-step through various ways to control the ship. You can not only use the VR touch controller, but also perform literal gestures with your virtual hands, as well as simply operate the steering wheel.

reviews.review_description: Very great game I have played loads of your games and have nearly every single one (mobile and vr ) please add some more ships and a way to get first rate In story mode as to what I know you cant. also more in depth ships like more customisation and go down to lower decks and be able to board in real time with muskets and bayonets and swords and then capture a ship like that. Also add more story and missions wise but make a little bit harder as it's is quite easy. But anyway absoulute great game like always !! Keep it up with this game . And there Is a bug with the st Mary's I think it is that is like blocked off when trying to get to the chest  please keep it up as this game could be even better 😀 👍!
Wonderful and exiting game with lots of opportunities and things to do. Well done.

This has instantly become one of my favorite games. The feeling of exploring the coastline of a world of the past while stumbling on small ports on a huge map is truly magical in my opinion and really well done (you can spend hours and only see a small portion of the map). The waves, the day and night circle including beautiful sunsets, watching the hugh amounted of palmtrees and landscapes in the distance. The moon mirroring in the water while sailing with another conquered ship following you is so soo well done. It's so relaxing enjoying the views while hearing the sound of the incredible looking waves. The fact that you can enter your cabin and through the windows can see the outside world (i wish the ship wouldn't stop sailing when entering the cabin) shows the amount of care that has gone into making this game. It's just so relaxing peaceful and beautiful. This is a world I enjoy being in (an aspect often underestimated but so important especially in VR games in my humble opinion). It reminds me of classic Sindbad movies. Wow. 5 stars and more.

Devs, great game.  Easy to get into and plunder the empires 😀. Sailing mechanics are good 👍. Love the open worldness.  The AI needs to be sprused up a bit.  The objects needs to be solid, but, you guys already know that 😉.  

If you like real pirate lore and game play. This is it. Totally worth getting it and support the devs to make improvements

Cheers ..

It would be really nice if you have a height setting in the settings cause I maxed out to the lowest height in meta and still cant reach stuff or be able to crouch down. Other thing would be really fun to have co-op with quests like the solo player.  I cant get my friend to join me for multi-player. Some reason he's in my room but nowhere to be found even they say they are in my room. I can see this game getting better.

Then an update dropped. It made a tedious change to map travel where you have to trace a line with joystick. I was going to suggest reverting it back, but a dev already responded that they were going to do just that, as an option. Wow, that shows the devs care about the experience rather than their own ego… you can tell a lot of time was put into that feature. Thanks for listening and responding!  I’m still learning the game, but really see a lot of potential and am enjoying it so far.

developer_response.body:
We make games for you, not to satisfy our own egos. If anything, your satisfaction gently strokes our egos. ;)

Download the newest version of the game - we’ve restored the fast travel option. After entering the cabin and choosing a location, check the "quick travel" box. The new map stays, because it allows you to discover new locations. Of course, the old ways of discovering places still work.

reviews.review_description:
This game is good in the 1 hour I have played it it was great except repairing the ships requires you to be on the ship I think you should be able to anchor it and have it get repaired while you explore. Otherwise this game is great.
Edit: nevermind the stuff about ship repair just had to progress through the game.

I changed my review to 5 stars after the 1.0 update they fixed pretty much all the jank and well they can still improve the game i highly recommend you buy this game it is one of the best pirate games ever made with tons of stuff to do i hope they keep improving it but its already so good
Worst game ever I can't play the game whith out bugs it lags so hard contacts don't respond have of the time on little boat can destroy like 3 of your big boats do not recommend it was so bad

This game satisfies the Jack Sparrow in me. Great graphics, artwork and sounds. The ocean, trees, underwater life, and caves are amazing. 

The only annoying thing about the game that you have to constantly calibrate your sextant with the lighthouses. It’s way too frequent and not fun to do. 

That being said this is a great game and lots of fun.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback and rating. The following tips should help.

To rescue the captain imprisoned in jail:
- Reach the port whose fort is holding the captive.
- Sail your Flagship as close to the fort as possible. Stop there.
- Confirm (A) the message that will start the mission inside the fort/prison.
- After freeing your comrade and returning to the room where you started, you will see a message. After confirming it, you will return to your ship.

How to use the bombs and attack the fort:
- Make sure that you have them in your cargo - use the B button, and then - "Go to Cabin" (point & confirm with trigger).
- Check "Manage Cargo" from the book and make sure that the ship you are using right now has them.
- Get back to your deck and switch to Battle Mode (press the right thumbstick or use the B => 'Battle mode').
- While in Battle mode, you can switch the ammo you are using by pressing the left thumbstick or using the B button, pointing at the ammo type that you want to use, and confirming with your right trigger.

You continue the fight as you would with any other ship. If you are unsure how to do this, make sure you have completed the tutorial and the quest ‘Bound by the Sea: Anne Bonny’. As a reference point, you can visit Nassau and check the PIRATES section in the book on the desk in your cabin.

Another place you might want to check is the "Controls Layout", available under the in-game settings, after pressing the menu (3 horizontal lines) button on your left touch controller. There you will find a comprehensive legend, which should become clear after a bit of experimenting with the interface and both of your touch controllers.

If you encounter any difficulties, please contact our support department using: https://www.homenetgames.com/support/

reviews.review_description: It's unplayable for me, because the game hasn't teleport function to move the main player. So I become dizzy right after 2 minutes of playing.
Yeah, when I was 14. my first Commodore64 game was the Pirates!. Now I am 50, I have a Quest3, and  the feeling is priceless. Take my money and give me mooooreee!!!

reviews.review_title: Needs a way of starting over.
reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this game, got me hooked in one day, and ive already got alot of progress. but in singleplayer, it needs a way to make a new game, and start over from scratch without doing some sketchy stuff. like have it so you can load older save files, then make a new game if you want to then go back to the older one when you feel like it (or delete older saves you don't want). otherwise this game is great (despite the occasional stutter here and there) so yea, this game needs a way to start a new game from scratch.

I haven't played long. I like the potential but the time when you're just sailing in the water with nothing there kind of boring. You should add more ships to be able to fight with. Maybe some more islands. I'd play if I can get an improvements.

reviews.review_title: Super addictive game needs a couple of
reviews.review_description:
Awesome addictive game. 
Wish they got more creative with secret chest of gold on land and under water dive sequences.

I bought this game and after an hour or so of playing I decided to refund it because I thought it was confusing and difficult to play, my refund was denied. I thought I might as well play a little bit more of the game because why not. I’m never happy when a refund gets denied but it showed me how truly amazing the game is once you learn the ropes.

I was so surprised by this game. The Graphics are great, the way the water and wind works together to move the ship, and all the other systems in this game make it so deep. I’ve seen people complain about what this game doesn’t have but to me it already does a lot and enough that everyone should be satisfied with. This one is definitely using all the power the Quest 3 can use.
Something is very wrong; the game constantly has low FPS or some kind of flickering issue. The idea is good, but it's very poorly optimized.

Been playing about 3 days now...love this game, the visuals, the songs, the first mate, sailing in 3rd person...trading, fighting, shooting cannon balls and grape shot...all awesome!

My only 2 gripes...1-a real tutorial would be great...so many times I had to just push buttons until something happened, or just keep trying stuff until it worked, and then hoped I knew what I did.
And 2...ship wreck diving seems to be broken...unless I'm in like 10-12 feet of water, then it seems to work ok.  If there is anything under me...it just keeps dunking my head under and popping me back out.

I'm hoping there is something I'm doing wrong...but the codex just says to dive underwater...no explanation on what to do.

After having seen some gameplay 6 months old, I was really very happy surprised how the game is now. Runs really smooth, and the navigation is excellent.
Could use a bit of guide to tell how to change canon direction during combat, and the quick change ammo also during combat.
But that is small stuff. I'm so happy I bought this today.. 👍

developer_response.body:
- To quickly switch between ship and battle mode, press the right analog stick.
- To change the cannon's direction during combat, press the Y button on your left controller.
- To quickly change ammo type during combat, press the left analog stick.

Apart from that, you might want to check the "Controls Layout", available under the in-game settings, after pressing the menu (3 horizontal lines) button on your left touch controller. There you will find a comprehensive legend, which should become clear after a bit of experimenting with the interface and both of your touch controllers.

reviews.review_title: Great game with some minor bugs
reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun and an easy way to make 8 hours feel like 10 minutes. My only complaint in that your fleet never works together and there’s no feature to set fleet tactics. When attacking a fort one ship does all the work while the others just hang out. You can set each individual ship to either “run from” or “attack” enemy ships but no way to control the fleet during combat. This is not a big deal until you’re trying to defeat a Port/Fort and have to spend all your earnings on repairs. Should be a simple fix. Great game otherwise.

Very cool game BUT it’s gets very repetitive I have some cool ideas that can make it much better
1: an option to buy ships and be able to buy other types of ships like a Galion 

2: the option to board a ship with your crew during a fight with a harpoon gun 

3: in towns and pirate hideout you actually have to go on the island and find the right people to buy stuff from and go to the tavern, in the tavern, you could buy rum and there’s also bar fights there, and you can only take up to one crew mate with you

4; guns and pistes that you fill up with fun poetry and then a bulleted

I've only had this game since yesterday and am a couple hours in - long enough to get the second ship, construct a couple buildings, complete a side quest or two...and it's fun. The combat is similar to AC Black Flag. Haven't gotten to the swordfighting or undersea / cave exploration yet. 

But I already want to give this five stars for one specific reason. The ocean is beautifully rendered, especially for the hardware they're working with. I'm surprised nobody else has mentioned this yet (or maybe it's just me), but it's done so well that I can "feel" the boat shifting under me as I sail in the first-person mode. It's a very unique experience. 

And as far as I can tell, the world truly is open, in that you don't *have* to "fast travel." Went to Port Howe, just set out across the ocean for 15 minutes or so, and yeah - the map moved. Not a lot, so the world is absolutely massive, but it moved. I just wish there was more out there. In that whole excursion, I only found one other ship and some wreckage.

reviews.review_title: Very bad and not worth it but graphics a
reviews.review_description: The game is slow and very hard to understand and you don’t do much in it it might look it but it’s just sitting at the top of a ship not worth the game

This is an interesting title.  I played it a little,  I had fun, but it's not pulling me back in necessarily. There really doesn't seem to be any story, just learn how to pirate within the confines of the game and off you go. It is fun, but it's just not as full fledged as I would like.

My first cave was a very involved adventure, I wasn't sure I'd ever make it out again. That concern was remedied when I fell through the solid floor and stood a ways below the level looking up at it, but unable to do anything as I was outside of the playable area. I didn't die either,  just hung out there for a few before turning the game off. It was definitely solid ground that I feel through too. I saw someone else say that and the developer responded as if the person had fallen down one of the many holes. I was extremely cautious as there are many intentional open pitfalls in the caves. That was not this, and I imagine I would have properly died too if that were the case and not just stood below looking up at the cave geometry.  

Aside from that glitch the game is fun, and I hope their keep adding and improving so that when I get back to it there will be more to keep me engaged.

developer_response.body:
We are constantly working on improving the game and fixing the bugs. 
If you can reproduce the issue that you encountered, and would be willing to guide us on how to reproduce it, please contact our support department using:
https://www.homenetgames.com/support/

reviews.review_description: We need to be able to get on islands, jump on enemy boats, and pirate combat
A lot of these games take some energy to even want to play. This one is chill and fun for just unwinding.
The game is very good and beautiful but it needs to be improved...thank you...we need more scenarios

reviews.review_title: Best game ive played in a while!
reviews.review_description: I just love how immersive it is and with you being able to lead your own fleet and complete the story mode however you want, I would definitely recommend this game for pirates of the Caribbean fans

reviews.review_title: Exceptional but not for the weezy
reviews.review_description:
This game is great so far. I haven't logged too many hours yet but there's quite a bit going on that it seems I'll have a lot of things to do. 

However, this game is really kicking in motion sickness more than most other titles I've tried so far. It was mild diving underwater, but moving in the caves for treasure nearly made me puke. I'm sure it's a me thing but I've never had that degree hit me before. 

That aside lol, I do feel like I acquired ships into my fleet too easily. I don't have a tavern yet and I'm stricken for gold yet I have 4 ships already in my group. That aspect just came too easily I think.

The game is beautiful so far, easily one of the better looking one's I've tried.

Looking forward to what other elements the dev's introduce. This is a five star game in truth; I'll update to five if I find some kind of in-game setting to reduce the motion sickness in caves (maybe offer an alternative navigation in those, similar to navigating on a map in your ship?)

Update: The game is now out of Early Access and therefore a full release.

 I've had a chance to play it and I have to say I really liked it. It has a great economic system and the ship battles are a lot of fun.

I'm already looking forward to the upcoming updates and especially the multiplayer!

You can see my first impressions of the game here:

https://youtu.be/0fmtpgpZ6HQ?si=03LF7hAQgqjwlnfI

Kaiser Lounge 😉

Highly recommended!
No dominant hand option, no button or stick swap. Inaccessible to a left handed player.

reviews.review_title: Simply the best game on quest 2.
reviews.review_description: There's so much to do in this game, it looks beautiful and most importantly, it's so much fun.

The single player has alot going on and is really cool and fun to play with not a triple A game but it's alot of fun where I find my self upset is the multiplayer I wanted a coop campaign and got ship only combat bots with friends or fight ur friends or fight a port with friends all only in your ship if you want a solo experience get it it's a good game for that but if you want a fun game with friends this game will only take up a couple hours of your time before you find your self wishing you could do more with your friends it's such a disappointment for a game with such a cool concept and ideas.
Is there a way to voice command hoisting the sails and the like?

Just started playing this game, and love it. Got to grips with the main functionality pretty quickly and now really looking forward to opening up the world.

Reminds me of Port Royale and Syd Myers pirates, but in glorious VR. And like my favourite VR games it’s immersive whilst being chilled. Don’t have to run around my front room, can sit down (when I’m in my cabin plotting my route )  and standup when I’m on the deck.

Going to put some hours in this week! Highly recommend!￼

This game could be a top title if the islands were accessible and you could swim when and where you wanted. Things like having a sword fight, swimming in sunken ships/caves and looking for air pockets to fill your lungs to carry on to find treasure would be good. Board enemy ships and have a fight.The islands don't need to be completely free roam but at least let us go alongside and have a walk about and do a bit of treasure hunting (x marks the spot and all that!) in the towns/beaches. It's a good game but just a bit shallow (excuse the pun).

developer_response.body:
We encourage you to download the new update. We’ve managed to implement most of your great ideas.
- The player character is no longer restricted. You can now jump off your ship and go ashore. Keep in mind that our goal is not to create an open-world RPG. Our main focus remains delivering a fun and relaxing pirate experience.
- Diving is now more natural. You swim by moving your hands, and interactions with chests and pearls feel more immersive. You use the GRAB button and perform natural gestures, such as pulling items toward yourself. Treasures tangled in ropes can be freed using a knife.
- Boarding national military ships can now result in sword combat on deck. Merchant ships, however, do not have soldiers on board who can fight back.
- Fast travel has been reworked. You now control your ship directly on the map, allowing you to choose your own route and discover the world on your own. Remember that every distance you sail comes at a cost.
- Barrels and crates are now retrieved using fishing nets. No more sailing up to floating cargo collected with the A button.
- Manual sextant calibration. Stop your ship near a lighthouse, take the sextant in your hand, bring it up to your eye, and look toward the lighthouse light.

reviews.review_description:
I've been playing the game for four hours now, and it's incredible how much of the spirit of the C64 game "Sid Meier's Pirates!" is captured in this game.

It's just fun to sail around the Caribbean, hunt down merchant ships, engage in battles with patrol ships and defeat their crews and captains in sword fights.

I hope that with the success of the game, it will get more and more features. It's definitely a hidden champion among the Meta Quest apps.

Update

I've been playing for several hours now, but I still haven't tried everything.


A few suggestions for improvement:

It should also be possible to sail from place to place in the world. For example, you should be able to switch back to the ship from the map at any time. 

Or the map could be scaled so that it takes about 2 minutes for one day. As an incentive, you could occasionally find clues to resources on the coast that you can reach by dinghy.

There should be storms on the map that you have to navigate around, otherwise the sails and hull will be damaged.

It should be possible to climb up to the lookout via the shrouds. I haven't managed to do that yet. I think it's great how you can run back and forth on the ship during combat, for example to aim the hunting cannons from the bow.

reviews.review_title: If you wanna be a pirate, get this game
reviews.review_description:
Love playing this game. Devs have been doing quite a few updates over the past six months. Ship combat takes some getting used to but you will get it down. Land mission combat is weak but I do like throwing daggers at everyone. 

Replace the first mate with a parrot. Haven't figured out how to sneak into the prison to rescue my comrade yet and the Discord doesn't seem to have a place to ask.
Great

The graphics in this game are fabulous. It took me a while to get to grips with the controls but that's probably because I'm not a six yr old, however, once I got going it was great fun. The pace is steady and the storyline keeps you interested. I've only scratched the surface but shall definitely launch myself into it properly, soon.

I like the music (please upload them to youtube) and the ship combat but that isnt suprising given that i spent about 778 days (according to the cloud save) in "the pirate: caribean hunt". There are some performance issues and the caves can be annoying because I wasnt abke to jump and the bridges where hard to get on top of due to this (might have been just a bug tho)


If the developers keep up the good work than it will defenitly become one of the best pirate games and maybe even the best pirate vr game (although there isnt as much competion)

This game has been a lot of fun, but in the latest update, instead of just being transported from one port to the next, and maybe being surprised by an enemy on the way I need to sail across a map. That means slowly tracing a line with a joystick to navigate my ships between ports. I much prefer the old way. It would be nice if sailing across the map was optional in the settings. Apart from that, this is one of my favorites.

developer_response.body: UPDATE: Download the newest version of the game - we’ve restored the fast travel option. After entering the cabin and choosing a location, check the "quick travel" box. The new map stays, because it allows you to discover new locations. Of course, the old ways of discovering places still work.

reviews.review_description: Awesome game just wish there was more hand to hand. And a way to buy ships. And more loot when you sink ships
There is so much to do it feels like a rich pirate experience.

The game knows what it want to deliver and delivers it perfectly. 

The gameplay variety is limited and so is the story but the game execute it's core mechanics really well. 

If you love sailing an open world and base building, you're going to love this game.
Still lacking a bit of content in the long run but a really well build game. Best pirate game currently out there!!!

Wicked little game love how it works the ships are very good love to see the working bits on the ship as you move about it's very good and will be looking forward to updates and addons hopefully!!!?!!!
